name: Brie and Pear Puff Pastry Tarts
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces brie cheese, at room temperature
1 pound firm ripe pears, peeled and sliced
1 egg mixed with 1 teaspoon water for wash
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Remove rind from brie cheese.
3. In a food processor, combine brie, egg, yolk, and brandy. Process until smooth and chill.
4. In a large skillet, melt butter. Add sugar and cook on high heat for 1 minute.
5. Add pears and cook over medium-high heat until crisp-tender. Remove to a plate and let cool.
6. On a floured surface, roll out one puff pastry sheet to a 10-inch square. Cut a 10-inch circle from this.
7. Roll out the second puff pastry sheet into an 11-inch square and cut an 11-inch circle from this.
8. Place the 10-inch pastry circle on a baking sheet. Spread cheese mixture over the pastry, leaving about 1 inch of dough at the edge.
9. Spoon the cooled pears on top of the cheese. Brush the 1-inch edge with egg glaze.
10. Score the 11-inch pastry circle halfway through with a sharp knife, without cutting all the way through the pastry.
11. Place the scored pastry on top of the pears and using a fork, seal the top pastry to the bottom.
12. Brush the surface with egg glaze. Vent the top with a knife and chill about 20 minutes before baking.
13. Bake at 450°F for 10 minutes, then turn down the heat to 375°F. Continue baking another 30 minutes or until puffy and golden brown.
14. Serve warm or at room temperature. If you don't want to use alcohol in this recipe, substitute brandy with brandy extract or your preferred flavoring.
